mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive 20-day returns when RSAFS is above its 12-month moving average is not significantly greater than 0.5 (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, it signals a tightening monetary stance that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, S&P 500 positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of cases, or the average forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment (UNRATE) and falling retail sales (RSAFS) signal an impending economic contraction, which compresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE rises month-over-month and RSAFS falls month-over-month simultaneously, the S&P 500 produces a positive 20-day return more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth above 3%), rising consumer prices erode real purchasing power and force central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns. In low inflation, rising prices reflect healthy demand and are positive for stocks, but this hypothesis tests only the high-inflation regime.
failure_condition: In triggered instances (high inflation), S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average negative return fails to be statistically significant (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant deterioration in industrial production, defined as INDPRO dropping more than 1% below its 3-month moving average, signals a weakening manufacturing sector that reduces corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered events, S&P 500 5-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment (PAYEMS) is rising and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is stable or declining, the economy is expanding without immediate tightening pressure, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where PAYEMS rises month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is unchanged or falling, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 2.0%), rising housing starts (HOUST) fuel residential investment and consumer wealth effects, boosting economic growth and equity returns. In higher rate regimes, housing demand is depressed and the positive signal weakens or reverses.
failure_condition: In triggered instances (low rates), S&P 500 60-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.5 percentage points over three months signals imminent economic weakness, hitting corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, S&P 500 60-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average negative return is not statistically significant (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
